# chore(phase-01): /analyze shard 2 — MVP objectives + USP refinement

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `5a2907985daf` — chore(phase-01): /analyze shard 2 — MVP objectives + USP refinement

**Body**:

Shard 2 of 25-shard /analyze plan. Two analytical deliverables that
gate every per-primitive deep-dive in shards 4–19.

02-mvp-objectives.md:
  Maps the 9 effective Phase 01 exit criteria (ROADMAP §59–65 plus
  the thesis §3.1 expansion the user accepted "AT ANY COSTS"). For
  each EC: the primitive that owns it, the BET it falsifies, the
  pre-declared acceptance gate, the failure-mode disposition. The
  6→9 expansion is operational unpacking, not scope creep — e.g.
  ROADMAP §63 "exports a verifiable hash-chained log" implies
  *some* verifier; thesis names it MUST be separately-codebased to
  prove the security primitive under adversarial assumption rather
  than developer assumption.

  Strongest acceptance gate: EC-9 (independent verifier).
  Verifier MUST share zero source code with producer; ideally a
  different language (Rust verifier for Python producer, per
  testing.md Tier 3 cross-implementation logic). Anything less is
  developer-trust theatre.

  Phase 01 ship predicate is strict conjunction of all 9 ECs; per
  zero-tolerance.md Rule 1 + Rule 6, "8 of 9" is not a release.
  External-gate clause is non-regression only — Phase 01 internal
  release does not require board endorsement; codename
  envoy-agent is acceptable for early-access pipx distribution.

02b-mvp-usps.md:
  CHARTER §47–59 lists 7 USPs; Phase 01 demonstrates 4 (U2
  Boundary Conversation, U3 Envoy Ledger, U4 Foundation
  stewardship, U6 no-orphan guarantee) and partially-or-not-at-all
  the other 3 (U1 ease of install needs P02 brew/winget/cargo, U5
  Python+Rust conformance needs both runtimes wired, U2a/U2b/U2c
  Boundary Library + Weekly + Monthly rituals are P02/P03).

  Names the demonstration strategy: each demonstrable USP rides on
  a specific EC which falsifies a specific BET. Aspirational USPs
  stay aspirational through P01 with an honest "Phase N target"
  framing. Includes the recommended P01 marketing line for shards
  19–20 packaging artifacts so they don't over-claim the phase
  ladder.

  U4 risk: positional USP (no feature gate) but vulnerable to
  silent regression — accidentally introducing commercial-entity
  references that contradict rules/independence.md. Mitigation:
  ship-gate gold-standards-validator review per agents.md.

Both docs cite Phase 00 sources by path + section per the
journal/0001 bridge pattern. No spec edits, no
frozen-contract artifact mutations.

Next entry point: shard 3 (kailash-py survey re-read +
primitive-provider table). Shard 3 has a pre-consumption
freshness gate per journal/0001 — must run gh issue list to
identify what's changed in kailash-py since the 2026-04-21 survey
before consuming it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
